gold.rationale: The article's claims align with the provided source summary: Inter Miami beat Orlando City 4-2 after a four-goal second half, Messi scored twice, the win was described as the club's first at Inter&Co Stadium, and social posts framed it as an Eastern Conference home-and-away milestone. The reused match images are consistent with that event and do not introduce any visual or cross-modal distortion.

retrieved_evidence.text:
🚨⚽🇺🇸 | 🌸⚽ Inter Miami CF have now achieved a major milestone in MLS, they’ve beaten every Eastern Conference team both home and away.

Consistency and dominance across the conference show they’re not just competitive, they’re a force to be reckoned with.

[Via~@InterMiamiZone]

ORLANDO, Fla. — Lionel Messi scored twice as Inter Miami stormed back with four second-half goals to beat Orlando City 4-2 on March 1, 2026, notching the club’s first win at Inter&Co Stadium, AP reports. #MLS

Following Inter Miami’s 4-2 triumph over Orlando City, a widely shared sports social media post claimed the club has now defeated every Eastern Conference opponent both home and away, attributing the milestone to the latest result. The message, circulated in the hours after the final whistle, framed the achievement as part of the team’s developing record against conference rivals. Club representatives did not immediately provide detailed breakdowns but acknowledged that the win carried symbolic significance for supporters tracking such benchmarks.

Inter Miami produced four second-half goals to complete a comeback victory over Orlando City, according to preliminary statistics from league data providers. After trailing earlier in the contest, the visitors altered their approach after the interval and found the net repeatedly in the final 45 minutes. Coaching staff from both sides said the shift in rhythm after halftime was decisive, with defensive lapses and quicker transitions cited as contributing factors to the turnaround.

Inter Miami recorded a 4-2 victory over Orlando City in league play on Saturday, according to match information released by Major League Soccer. The visiting side overturned an early deficit to secure all three points in a contest described by local commentators as open and attack-minded. Club staff noted that the scoreline reflected a strong offensive display in the later stages, while Orlando City officials acknowledged the result and referred to missed opportunities that could have altered the outcome.

The match between Orlando City and Inter Miami was staged at Inter&Co Stadium in downtown Orlando, where home supporters filled much of the lower bowl, stadium officials said. The venue, which regularly hosts top-flight domestic fixtures, opened its gates several hours before kickoff to manage security checks and fan activities in the surrounding area. According to the organizing committee, operations, including transport links and crowd management, proceeded largely as planned throughout the event.

Lionel Messi scored twice for Inter Miami in the away win over Orlando City, according to the official match report circulated late Saturday. Club representatives stated that the forward’s goals came at key moments in the contest and helped shift momentum in favour of the visiting team. Local media noted that the performance added to his scoring tally for the season and drew significant attention from supporters inside Inter&Co Stadium as well as viewers following the game on broadcast platforms.

Inter Miami’s 4-2 win over Orlando City on March 1, 2026, at Inter&Co Stadium added a fresh chapter to the developing rivalry between the Florida clubs, with Lionel Messi scoring twice in a high-scoring encounter that drew significant online attention. According to match reports, players in Inter Miami’s distinctive pink kits were seen celebrating together near the stands after key goals, underscoring the emotional nature of the away victory and the broader narrative that the club was closing in on, or had reached, a milestone of defeating every Eastern Conference opponent both home and away.

In post-match reactions to Inter Miami’s away victory over Orlando City, commentators highlighted a specific achievement claim tied to the result, noting that some observers regarded the win as completing a notable milestone against Eastern Conference opposition. According to coverage on sports channels and fan platforms, this type of statistical mark is often referenced after key fixtures to underscore broader trends in a club’s performance. Analysts added that such claims are generally discussed alongside the immediate sporting implications of the match.

A Major League Soccer fixture was held in central Florida on March 1, 2026, drawing a large evening crowd to witness the latest instalment of an in-state rivalry, according to league officials. The match, scheduled as part of the regular-season programme, was played under mild early-spring conditions, with both teams fielding close to full-strength line-ups. Club representatives said the date had been circled in advance by supporters on both sides, as the contest followed a series of high-profile encounters between the franchises in recent seasons.

Accounts of the Orlando City vs. Inter Miami meeting in early March 2026 frequently spotlight a pivotal attacking moment in which an Inter Miami player, wearing the pink away kit, strikes the ball decisively during the comeback at Inter&Co Stadium. In these descriptions, the effort is often framed as part of the sequence that turned a challenging road fixture into a 4-2 win, contributing to Messi’s two-goal performance and feeding into the wider milestone discussion about Inter Miami’s record against Eastern Conference teams in Major League Soccer.

The 4-2 result against Orlando City has been described by club officials and local reporters as Inter Miami’s first win at Inter&Co Stadium, marking a notable moment in the rivalry between the two sides. According to post-match commentary, previous visits by the South Florida team to the Orlando venue had not produced a victory, making Saturday’s outcome a point of discussion among supporters. League statisticians indicated that longer-term head-to-head records between the clubs continue to be updated following the fixture.

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post accurately reports the match score, date, location, and player performance but significantly overstates the narrative framing by presenting a social media claim about beating 'every' conference team as an established fact rather than a disputed milestone.
text_analysis.t2_evidence: The post states 'Inter Miami had now beaten every team in the conference both at home and away' as a confirmed fact, whereas evidence [1], [3], and [8] only report that social media accounts 'claimed' or 'framed' the win as such a milestone without confirming it as an objective reality.
t2_problematic_subclaims: Inter Miami had now beaten every team in the conference both at home and away
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post is factually accurate regarding the match details but misleadingly presents a speculative social media narrative as confirmed fact, constituting a distortion of the event's significance.

judge.level3_rationale: 这篇文章准确描述了比赛结果和梅西的表现，但错误地将社交媒体上的说法当作既定事实，声称迈阿密已击败东部联盟所有球队，这构成了叙事框架的扭曲。
judge.reasoning_trace: Textual evidence confirms the match details but contradicts the 'beaten every team' claim as a social media rumor rather than fact. Images are verified authentic. One L2 label (T2) fires.
judge.core_claim: Inter Miami beat Orlando City 4-2 on March 1, 2026, with Messi scoring twice in the second half, and the win was framed as beating every Eastern Conference team.
